title: Settings with Default Members
prompt_md:
## Goal
Define a `Settings` struct whose members have default member initializers, then create objects that either fall back to those defaults or override them.

The struct has two members: `label` (default `"General"`) and `volume` (default `50`).

## Input
- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 5), the number of settings objects.
- Next `N` lines: each is either the word `default` (use all defaults) or a label word followed by an integer volume (override both).

## Output
For each object, one line:
```
<label>: volume=<volume>
```

## Constraints
- Give both members default member initializers in the struct definition. A `default` line must produce a `Settings` built with empty braces so the defaults apply; do not re-type the default values yourself.

test_cases.label: Sample -- mix of default and custom
test_cases.expected_stdout:
General: volume=50
Music: volume=80
General: volume=50

test_cases.label: Hidden -- all custom with boundary values

title: Partial Override with Default Member Initializers
prompt_md:
## Goal
Define a `Character` struct where several stats have sensible defaults, then create characters that override only some of those stats using designated initializers.

The struct has four members with defaults: `name` = `"Default"`, `health` = `100`, `attack` = `15`, `defense` = `10`.

## Input
- Line 1: a single-word character name.
- Line 2: an integer health value (1-1000).

## Output
Two lines:
- The custom character (name and health from input, but `attack` and `defense` keep their defaults):
  ```
  <name>: hp=<health> atk=<attack> def=<defense>
  ```
- A fully default character (created with empty braces):
  ```
  Default: hp=100 atk=15 def=10
  ```

## Constraints
- Provide a default member initializer for every member.
- Build the custom character with designated initializers setting only `name` and `health`; `attack` and `defense` must come from the defaults, not values you write again.

test_cases.label: Sample -- hero with custom name and health
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Archer: hp=250 atk=15 def=10
Default: hp=100 atk=15 def=10

test_cases.label: Hidden -- minimum health
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Skeleton: hp=1 atk=15 def=10
Default: hp=100 atk=15 def=10

test_cases.label: Hidden -- high health value
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Dragon: hp=1000 atk=15 def=10
Default: hp=100 atk=15 def=10
